methods.name: AddPictureFrame
methods.signature: public Guid AddPictureFrame(Plane plane, string texturePath, bool asMesh, double width, double height, bool selfIllumination, bool embedBitmap)
methods.summary: Creates a PictureFrame object from a plane and a path to an image file, Note, a PictureFrame object is just a Plane surface or mesh that has a material with a texture assigned to it that displays in all display modes.

params.name: plane
params.doc: Plane in which the PictureFrame will be created. Bottom left corner of picture will be at plane's origin, width will be in the plane's x axis direction, height will be in the plane's y axis direction.

params.name: asMesh
params.doc: If true, the function will make a MeshObject rather than a surface

params.name: width
params.doc: Width of the resulting PictureFrame. If 0.0, the width of the picture frame is the width of the image if height is also 0.0 or calculated from the height and aspect ratio of the image if height is not 0.0.

params.name: height
params.doc: Height of the resulting PictureFrame. If 0.0, the height of the picture frame is the height of the image if width is also 0.0 or calculated from the width and aspect ratio of the image if width is not 0.0.

params.name: selfIllumination
params.doc: If true, the image mapped to the picture frame plane always displays at full intensity and is not affected by light or shadow.

params.name: embedBitmap
params.doc: If true, the function adds the image to the bitmap table of the document to which the PictureFrame will be added

returns.doc: A unique identifier for the object

methods.name: Find
methods.signature: public RhinoObject Find(uint runtimeSerialNumber)
methods.summary: Use the object runtime serial number to find a rhino object in the document. This is the value stored on RhinoObject.RuntimeObjectSerialNumber. The RhinoObject constructor sets the runtime serial number and every instance of a RhinoObject class will have a unique serial number for the duration of the Rhino application. If an object is replaced with a new object, then the new object will have a different runtime serial number. Deleted objects stored in the undo list maintain their runtime serial numbers and this function will return pointers to these objects. Call RhinoObject.IsDeleted if you need to determine if the returned object is active or deleted. The runtime serial number is not saved in files.

params.name: runtimeSerialNumber
params.doc: Runtime serial number to search for.

returns.doc: Reference to the rhino object with the objectId or null if no such object could be found.

methods.name: FindId
methods.signature: public override RhinoObject FindId(Guid id)
methods.summary: Uses the object guid to find a rhino object. Deleted objects cannot be found by id. The guid is the value that is stored on RhinoObject.Id In a single document, no two active objects have the same guid. If an object is replaced with a new object, then the guid persists. For example, if the _Move command moves an object, then the moved object inherits it's guid from the starting object. If the Copy command copies an object, then the copy gets a new guid. This guid persists through file saving/opening operations. This function will not find grip objects.

params.name: id
params.doc: ID of object to search for.

returns.doc: Reference to the rhino object with the objectId or null if no such object could be found.
